ticket.subject: New customer message on 5 May 2026 at 23:41
ticket.customerBody:
You received a new message from your online store's contact form.

Country Code:
DE

Name:
Lukas Juelshøj

Email:
Lukasbj2002@icloud.com

Company / Team:

Your Country:
Danmark

If Applicable, Place Of Purchase And Order Number:
Date 26-04-2026 country Denmark Order #4350

What Is Your Request Regarding?:
A-Spire Wireless

What Do You Need Help With?:
Other

Body:
Dear AceZone Support,

I am writing to request a return and refund for my recently
purchased A-Spire Wireless headset (Order #4350).

After testing the product, I have decided that it does not meet
my expectations. Specifically, I am not satisfied with the
overall feel and comfort of the headset. At times, it feels like
there are pressure points or “spikes,” which makes it
uncomfortable to wear for longer sessions. Additionally, I have
experienced that the battery seems to drain relatively quickly.

I am also not fully satisfied with the microphone quality, as it
does not consistently perform at the level I expected.

Based on these factors, I would like to proceed with a return and
refund in accordance with your 30-day return policy. Please let
me know the next steps and confirm whether my return request is
approved.

Thank you for your assistance.

Kind regards,
Lukas

results.draft:
Hi Lukas,

Thanks for reaching out. I’m sorry the A-Spire Wireless isn’t meeting your expectations.

We can start your return for order #4350. Please send the headset and all accessories in original condition to:
AceZone International ApS
Øster Allé 56, 5th floor
2100 København Ø
Denmark

Include “Order #4350” inside the parcel. Once we receive and check the return, we’ll issue the refund.

I look forward to hearing from you once it’s shipped, and you can share the tracking link here in the thread.

ticket.subject: Re: [AceZone] Re: New customer message on 2 May 2026 at 03.17
ticket.customerBody:
Or could it be time to get a new headset after 2 years? I feel like  the
sound got a bit worse vs when I got it 2 years ago.  🫠

Den tirs. 5. maj 2026 kl. 20.29 skrev Simon Dec <simondec2021@gmail.com>:

results.draft:
Hi there,

Thanks for reaching out, I get how frustrating it feels when sound quality seems to drop over time.

Before considering a new headset, let’s rule out a few common causes that can make audio seem worse on Windows:

- Disable Windows audio enhancements:
  1) Press Win+R, type mmsys.cpl, press Enter
  2) Playback tab → right‑click your headset → Properties
  3) Enhancements tab → check “Disable all enhancements” → Apply
  4) Spatial sound tab → set to Off → Apply
- Check balance: In the same Properties window, Levels → Balance, make sure left/right are equal.
- Try a factory reset of the headset (this often clears odd audio behavior). If you’re unsure of the steps for your model, let me know and I’ll share them for you.
- Test a different USB port/cable or Bluetooth source, and try in a different room to rule out interference.

If none of that helps, it could be a hardware issue rather than normal aging. Tell me how it goes with the steps above, and we’ll take the next step.

I look forward to hearing from you.

missing_for_10: next steps for warranty or replacement if issues persist
qa.reasoning: The response is thorough in troubleshooting but lacks a clear commitment to warranty or replacement options if the issue is not resolved.

ticket.subject: Re: [AceZone] Re: New customer message on 4 May 2026 at 12:21
ticket.customerBody:
HI,

I applied this fix but unfortunately, I just had another instance where it disconnects and reconnects mid game again. Are there any other fixes I could possibly try?

Best regards,
Nathan
________________________________
Van: AceZone Support (Support) <support@acezone.io>
Verzonden: maandag 4 mei 2026 12:29
Aan: Nathan Segers <nathan.segers@hotmail.com>
Onderwerp: [AceZone] Re: New customer message on 4 May 2026 at 12:21

Hi Nathan,

Thank you for reaching out.

It seems that something may be interfering with your connection.
Could you inform me of how you have the dongle set up? - Is it placed at the back or top of your PC, and how far away are you sitting?

I recommend doing a factory reset of the headset + resetting dongle:

To do a factory reset on the A-Blaze:


  1.  Turn the headset off.
  2.  Press and hold power button for 15 seconds.
  3.  A voice prompt will tell you a factory reset has been made.


Please note that this will delete all saved Bluetooth connections from your headset, including your phone/PC and dongle. You should also delete the headset from your phone/PC's saved devices, as well as delete it from the AceZone app before pairing again. We'll need to re-pair the headset and the dongle:


  1.  Plug the dongle into your PC.
  2.  Reset the dongle by holding down the button until the LED turns purple.
  3.  Remove the dongle.
  4.  Turn your headset on, and make sure no cables are connected.
  5.  Hold the Noise Cancelling mode button (right next to the power button) for 4 seconds to enable pairing mode.
  6.  Plug the dongle back in. The LED will blink blue/red.
  7.  Pairing will happen automatically. The LED will turn blue when the pairing is done.


Make sure that when you are using dongle connection, try to disable bluetooth on your mobile device if you have had it connected previously.
Furthermore, please open your Mobile App and disable Standby Timer under System Settings 🙂

Let me know if any of these methods worked out!

Have a lovely day.

________________________________
Venlig Hilsen / Kind Regards,

[AceZone Logo]
Elias Knudsen
Support Associate
Acezone.io<http://acezone.io/>

This email is a service from AceZone. Delivered by Zendesk<https://www.zendesk.com/support/?utm_campaign=text&utm_content=AceZone&utm_medium=poweredbyzendesk&utm_source=email-notification>

results.draft:
Hi Nathan,

Thanks for the update. Since it’s still disconnecting mid‑game, let’s try a few targeted steps to rule out interference and USB/driver issues. Please skip anything you already did.

- Dongle placement: Use a front USB port or a short USB extension to bring the dongle away from the back of the PC and other antennas. Keep it in clear line‑of‑sight to the headset and at least 20–30 cm away from Wi‑Fi routers, PC case, and metal surfaces.
- Disable other connections: While using the dongle, turn off Bluetooth on nearby devices that have been paired to the headset (phone/tablet/PC) and avoid using the USB‑C cable at the same time. In the AceZone app, keep Standby Timer off during testing.
- Reinstall Windows USB audio drivers for the headset: With the headset powered on and connected via USB‑C, open Device Manager → Sound, video and game controllers → find the headset → right‑click → Uninstall device. Unplug the headset, then plug it back into the same USB port to reinstall the driver.
- Fresh dongle pairing: Reset the dongle (hold the button until the LED turns purple), remove it, power the headset on, hold the NC button for ~4 seconds to enter pairing mode, then reinsert the dongle. The LED blinks blue/red and turns solid blue when paired.
- Test on another USB port and, if possible, another PC to compare stability.

If the issue persists after these steps, please let me know exactly where the dongle is placed and how far you sit from it, plus whether the drop happens only in specific games/apps or system‑wide. I’ll escalate with those details.

I look forward to hearing from you.

qa.reasoning: The response is factually accurate and includes all necessary troubleshooting steps, but could benefit from a slightly warmer closing to enhance customer experience.

ticket.subject: Re: [AceZone] Re: Fwd: [AceZone] Re: Issue with connection
ticket.customerBody:
Hey!

I have finally received my headset. But the second I put it on my head and
turned it on I heard a constant sort of rumbling sound, almost like someone
is blowing air in my left ear. It's definitely different from before I sent
it, but equally as distracting, and in the same ear.

Is this something you're aware of, and is there anything I'm doing wrong?
The sound persists regardless of whether it's connected to my PC by cable,
bluetooth, or not connected at all. It rises in volume for a few seconds,
then gradually goes down to a low rumble, before rising again.

I didn't have this issue when I first got the headset, and not with the
stand-in headset I loaned.

If you could be so kind as to give me a list of things to check, I'll get
straight to it.

Best regards,
Simen Valhaug.

On Fri, 1 May 2026, 09:09 AceZone Support (Support), <support@acezone.io>
wrote:

results.draft:
Hi Simen,

Thanks for reaching out, and I’m sorry about the persistent rumbling in the left ear. Let’s run through a focused checklist to rule out software settings and then reset the headset:

1) Windows sound checks (skip if you’re on Mac):
- Set the headset as the default output device.
- Open mmsys.cpl → Playback → your headset → Properties → Enhancements → Disable all enhancements → Apply.
- Go to Spatial sound → Off → Apply.
- In the Levels/Balance controls, make sure left/right are both at 100 and centered.

2) Test on multiple sources:
- Try a phone and a different PC, both over Bluetooth and via cable, just to confirm it’s independent of the source (you already indicated it persists, but this confirms after the changes above).

3) Factory reset the headset:
- Power the headset off.
- Hold the main power button for 10–15 seconds until you see the reset indication (LED/prompt), then release.
- Forget/remove the headset on your devices and re-pair if you test Bluetooth again.

4) If using any comms apps (Discord, etc.):
- Disable KRISP or other noise/voice enhancements and close any third‑party EQ or sound-improvement tools.

If the rumble continues after the full reset and settings checks, we’ll treat this as a warranty case and proceed with a swap. Just let me know the result once you’ve completed the steps above, and we’ll move forward right away.

I look forward to hearing from you.

missing_for_10: commit to warranty process and provide tracking link if applicable
qa.reasoning: The response is thorough in troubleshooting steps but lacks a commitment to warranty action if the issue persists.

ticket.subject: Subject: Case #5882 – UPS fortollingskrav 1031 NOK (A‑Spire Wireless erstatning)
ticket.customerBody:
UPS-sporingsnr.: V59 21X 68 1093 3388
UPS/ACEZONE-ref.: ACEZONE19031257
Dette headsettet er reklamasjons sak og jeg betalte ikke for det.
Hilsen Marie Frøge

results.draft:
Hej Marie

Tak for din tilbagemelding. Noteret: UPS-sporingsnummer V59 21X 68 1093 3388 og ref. ACEZONE19031257. Det er registreret som en garantisag, og du skal ikke betale for den.

Jeg tjekker status på forsendelsen hos UPS nu og følger op, så sagen kommer videre uden omkostninger for dig. Hvis der mangler noget fra deres side, sørger vi for at få det på plads.

Undskyld for ulejligheden og tak for din tålmodighed.
